item.title: Donald Trump: How his high-stakes meeting with Xi Jinping was covered in the Chinese press
item.description: While Donald Trump's Beijing visit was being portrayed in the West as one of an unpredictable man ready to settle the some of the region's toughest issues, the Chinese press remained blank-faced and stoic about the entire affair, keeping its focus on its own hospitality and interests.

item.title: One Belt, One Road: China heralds 'Digital Silk Road'; foresees internet-era power shift soon
item.description: Cybertechnology leaders from around the world gather in China for an annual internet conference, but two key elements of this years event — the establishment of Beijing's Digital Silk Road and the attendance of American technology trailblazers — mark a potential shift in global digital power dynamics.

item.title: "Strong evidence" of citrus canker in NT
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chinese rabies vaccine scandal sparks outrage among authorities and public
item.description: China's regulator orders a vaccine manufacturer to recall all its products after finding it violated standards in the production of a rabies vaccine, in a breach that has angered authorities and the public.

item.title: #MeToo in China: Buddhist monk resigns, TV star sues as movement makes a comeback
item.description: A high-ranking Buddhist monk, a famous television presenter and the head of an NGO are some of the latest men to be publicly accused of sexual offences in China.

item.title: Spotlight forum: the transition to clean energy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Students and parents demand answers after at least two high schools hand out wrong paper in VCE Chinese exam
item.description: Students and parents are calling for more transparency and adequate solutions from the VCE authority after at least two high schools gave Chinese language students the wrong exam paper this week.

item.title: VCE students say Chinese language exam amended after being leaked was then missing audio component
item.description: Victorian Certificate of Education (VCE) students who were forced to take an amended Chinese language exam after it was accidentally given to a different class complain of a further stuff-up with the test.

item.title: Reserve Bank restructure closer to reality after Treasurer Jim Chalmers offers olive branch to Angus Taylor
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia is a step closer to its biggest restructure in three decades after the treasurer offers an assurance to his Coalition counterpart.

item.title: Free beach wellness gatherings caught up in councils' red tape
item.description: Organisers say they are trying to promote wellness, peace, and connection within their communities through free activities. Then came the council bill.

item.title: Watching Apple Cider Vinegar filled me with rage and clarity: The answer to Belle Gibson's lies is science
item.description: If we want to avoid the potentially fatal sway of canny fabulists like Belle Gibson, the answer is not to ridicule patients but to elevate science, to put more money into cancer research.

item.title: Artist Minna Gilligan is learning to be kind to herself as a first-time parent
item.description: The artist shares the "little ways" she gains a sense of control over the chaos of motherhood.

item.title: How chef Brian Cole juggles late nights, stressful shifts and fatherhood
item.description: Brian Cole says his mother wanted him to become a doctor or a lawyer. He became a chef instead.

item.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
item.description: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

item.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
item.description: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

item.title: How white nationalists infiltrated the wellness industry
item.description: On the surface, they seem like typical soap businesses. But to their audience, they are a tool for spreading hate.

item.title: Fight against fire ants at tipping point
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Here's what nutrition and diet experts say about green powders
item.description: Green powders might seem like the answer to lacklustre veggie consumption, but are they actually worth taking?

item.title: Australia's first Chinese WWII comfort women statue searches for permanent home
item.description: Though hailed as a "beautiful call for peace", this 60kg bronze statue of a young girl has languished in a suburban garage for nearly a year.

item.title: Doctors say Chinese Australians should get checked for breast cancer earlier. Here's why they don't
item.description: As Breast Cancer Awareness Month draws to a close, support groups urge Chinese women to get screened for breast cancer, as doctors highlight how the disease impacts this cohort differently.

item.title: Locusts in Victoria
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Doctors say more packaged foods should have health star ratings
item.description: Health bodies want star ratings on all packaged foods to help shoppers make healthier choices, but the grocery industry says the voluntary system works.

item.title: Mystery blue streak spotted over southern Queensland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: China's Zhuque-2E Y6 rocket launches two new satellites into orbit
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World's largest whale graveyard found in the Indian Ocean
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Short on space? These are veggies you can grow indoors and on balconies
item.description: Even if you're short on outdoor space, there are ways to grow your own food at home. Here's what the experts recommend.

item.title: Superhero Day at Wagga schools for rare Schimke immuno-osseous dysplasia
item.description: Nate Lawson's family looked to their son's love of superheroes to raise awareness about the rare disease that took his life.

item.title: Love story: How a meet-cute led to a throuple romance
item.description: Everyone is bisexual, polyamorous, and has a crush on me. Or at least, that's my default assumption and the pick-up line that led to my triad with Rhea and Leo.

item.title: The fake ABC News articles trying to sell you a scam
item.description: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

item.title: Will SpaceX bring stocks thudding back to earth?
item.description: As we plough on through the fourth consecutive year of global stock records and huge annual advances, it's not surprising that many pundits have begun to question just how much longer this boom can continue.

item.title: Multicultural communities still struggle with emergency messaging in times of crisis
item.description: Multicultural communities may not automatically understand the intent behind Australia's emergency messaging, but there are ways to fix this.

item.title: Health warning issued after powerful sedative etomidate was found in illegal vapes in Victoria
item.description: Victorian health authorities say vape products containing the powerful sedative etomidate have been found being sold illegally.

item.title: Indian medical exam leaks scandal sparks a resit, protests and tragedy
item.description: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

item.title: Gilmour Space Technologies' rocket launch ended shortly after lift-off
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Housing Minister denies tax changes are major driver for market 'correction'
item.description: Clare O'Neil has acknowledged that the property market is undergoing a correction, but says the government's proposed tax changes are not the sole cause.

item.title: Spotting the signs to stop the 'slippery slope' of gambling addiction
item.description: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

item.title: Australia’s heatwave history offers lessons for sweltering Europe
item.description: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has highlighted its vulnerability to climate change. But countries could look to Australia's experience with scorching summers to learn valuable lessons.

item.title: Developer gets green-light to turn Sydney boarding house into luxury homes after tenants rehoused
item.description: A rundown boarding house which was once a haven for vulnerable people in inner Sydney is set to be converted into luxury apartments.

item.title: Military-grade thermal imaging and helicopters were deployed to achieve the complete eradication of feral pigs from Australia's third-largest island.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Moist Mondays' help young Australians take the plunge and make new friends
item.description: Young people are turning to local community groups to get out of their comfort zones, make real connections and boost their wellbeing.

item.title: How Karl Stefanovic went from Today show glory to 'Joe Bogan' podcaster
item.description: Today show host Karl Stefanovic is reportedly leaving Channel Nine after a more than 20-year career at the network that was filled with highs and lows.

item.title: Hanson's increased visibility comes at a price for One Nation
item.description: Parliament House has been Pauline's World lately but increased visibility comes with increased scrutiny and this week revealed that One Nation is not untouchable.

item.title: Government and union endorse 16 per cent pay rise offer for SA nurses and midwives
item.description: Nurses and midwives in South Australia would receive a 16 per cent pay rise by mid-2028 under a new deal endorsed by their union and the state government.

item.title: The deadly nitrous oxide flooding into Australia
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: What can Australia learn from Asia's battle with H5N1 variant bird flu?
item.description: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

item.title: Tele-oncology units at West Moreton hospitals cut cancer patients' travel time
item.description: Cancer patients in Brisbane's rural outskirts have saved thousands of kilometres of travel to access oncology services.

item.title: Today show host Karl Stefanovic to leave Channel Nine, network confirms
item.description: Today show host Karl Stefanovic will leave Channel Nine after a controversial podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson.

item.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation after ship reports attack
item.description: The UN pauses its operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel reports an attack.

item.title: WA farming town turns to 3D printing to house its migrant workers
item.description: With a wealth of job opportunities but no houses for skilled workers, a regional WA town is hoping 3D-printed homes can solve the shortage.

item.title: Queensland man still chasing sailing dreams despite terminal illness
item.description: David Burston had only just bought an old yacht to fix up and sail around Australia when he was diagnosed with stage 4 cancer. Hee has decided to keep pursuing his dream for as long as he can.

item.title: Will bird flu in Australia affect the price of eggs?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Operators cancel cuttlefish season resulting in million-dollar losses
item.description: Whyalla has been forced to wind back services and let staff let go as its tourism industry suffers million-dollar losses from cuttlefish not making their usual migration.

item.title: Echuca Moama Taxi Group fined over deaths of two passengers in wheelchairs
item.description: A Victorian taxi company has been fined over the separate 2021 deaths of two passengers who used wheelchairs.

item.title: Canberra wildlife sanctuary builds quarantine pens amid fears H5 bird flu could 'decimate' quoll population
item.description: A population of endangered eastern quolls is being protected by a sanctuary in Canberra, but the threat of bird flu could mean that protection does not go far enough.

item.title: US strikes Iran after Trump calls attack in Strait of Hormuz a 'foolish' ceasefire breach
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards vows a "swift and decisive" response, according to Iranian state television, claiming they repelled a US attack on Sirik Island, on the shores of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: How on earth has the global economy survived this ongoing war?
item.description: An unfortunate side effect of the incompetent and erratic negotiating tactics of the Trump administration is that some of the underlying stories in the global economy can get overlooked.

item.title: H5N1 bird flu a deadly threat to native duck species already menaced from all angles
item.description: Australia's native duck species were already threatened by urban sprawl, climate change, hunting and habitat degradation. Now H5N1 bird flu has been discovered on our shores.

item.title: Olive growers excited as consumer demand rises, industry looks to expand
item.description: Consumer interest in olive oil has boomed at the same time as growing the produce has flourished, leading to excitement in the industry.

item.title: Art 'like medicine' for accountant turned painter after brain injury
item.description: After undergoing brain surgery, Cheryl Blacklock can no longer process numbers quickly and has instead found a new life through art, painting faces that appear as visions in her mind.

item.title: World Cup: Ousmane Dembélé suspends time as France's favouritism heightens
item.description: Ousmane Dembélé showed patience and a command of time and space, to help propel France to the top of its group, and remain on course for World Cup glory.

item.title: Rich and famous families teach their kids Mandarin as China's influence grows
item.description: Rich and powerful families have a strong interest in one particular language, with experts saying it is no surprise given the country's rise.

item.title: Citizen scientists say hotline told them to collect dead bird against government advice
item.description: South Australian citizen scientists say they called the Emergency Animal Disease Hotline to report a dead sea bird and were given advice that contradicts official government messaging.

item.title: Iran says it hit US-linked targets as Bahrain reports drone attack
item.description: Also in the Strait of Hormuz an oil tanker has been damaged by an "unidentified projectile", while an Israeli drone has reportedly struck an area of southern Lebanon.

item.title: Karl Stefanovic's 'weird personality' might score him a Logie
item.description: Karl Stefanovic is no longer part of the Nine family but his audience may not be ready to abandon their prodigal son.

item.title: Vance vs Rubio: Which White House apprentice will win Trump's backing for 2028?
item.description: At times, Donald Trump appears to be harking back to his days hosting reality TV show The Apprentice as he leans into speculation about who he will anoint as his successor.

item.title: The truth about narcissists — and why most people get it wrong
item.description: As "narcissist" becomes the catch-all insult of the social media age, here is the crucial difference between everyday selfishness and a serious personality disorder.

item.title: Lindy Chamberlain murder trial artefacts and memorabilia paint picture of frenzy around 1982 case
item.description: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

item.title: Central Queensland family's 'heartbreaking' fight against motor neurone disease
item.description: A Queensland family shares its journey with motor neurone disease for the first time and gives advice on how to cope with a diagnosis.

item.title: Parents of children with amputations turn to online communities for accessible clothing
item.description: Braxton's mum says there needs to be more clothing options available for children with amputations, after struggling to find some for her son whose leg became amputated in the womb.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she has growing support in Canberra. But does it pass the pub test?
item.description: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

item.title: Young people from Australian and American cities find a home in outback Queensland
item.description: After escaping the big smoke, these five young people have swapped the metro rat race for a slower life in the outback – and say they wouldn’t change a thing.

item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes as Gulf tensions escalate despite ceasefire
item.description: The United States and Iran have exchanged strikes against targets in the Middle East, underscoring rising tensions that threaten the interim deal between the two nations to stop the war.

item.title: Successful cat trapping effort leads to spike in Tasmanian devil population
item.description: A successful feral cat trapping effort in northern Tasmania leads to a spike in the devil population.

item.title: Beyond the Shed training program prepares WA shearers for their future and a stronger local workforce
item.description: Almost 100 shearing shed workers across regional WA sign up to a new training program aimed at helping contractors and their teams find work beyond the shearing industry.

item.title: Treasurer Jim Chalmers promises to 'fix' property tax oversight to protect widows and divorcees
item.description: The Treasurer is promising to "fix" an unintended consequence of the government's overhaul of property tax breaks to protect widows and divorcees — but he has refused to say exactly how.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Salah's injury a Socceroos boost, Scotland's coach resigns and the tightest of offsides
item.description: The Socceroos' next World Cup opponents Egypt are sweating on the fitness of key striker Mohamed Salah, Scotland's coach Steve Clarke resigns after his team's exit and Davinson Sánchez was denied a late winner because of his toe. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Young station ringers win Battle of the Barkly at the NT outback's Brunette Races
item.description: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

item.title: The surprising reasons some bosses oppose work from home
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.
